reasoning: Operate metrics are mocked; the real next move is a one-week validation sprint with paid SKU packs and revision economics.
known: No real operating metrics exist yet.
A first-week scorecard can be defined before running the experiment.
assumed: Direct outreach will produce enough replies to test paid intent.
Revision count and image acceptance will determine service viability.
toValidate: Actual paid conversion from seller outreach.
Actual GPT Image 2 cost and operator time per SKU pack.
Actual seller satisfaction and repeat-intent after delivery.
concerns: Mocked metrics cannot justify build investment.
If revision count is high, the service may become a low-margin agency.
If product fidelity fails, the whole wedge weakens.
nextSteps: Record every seller reply, call, payment, delivery, and revision.
Re-run validate and business-model stages with real evidence.
Only build automation for repeated manual steps.

analysis.biggestBlocker: No real seller payment or delivery data yet.

nextExperiment.name: 5 seller paid SKU pack sprint
nextExperiment.goal: Prove sellers will pay for a reviewed Amazon-ready image pack.
nextExperiment.successMetric: 2 paid packs and average revision count under 2

decisionTreeResult.recommendation: go back to validation with paid manual service

handoffSummary.summary: Replace mock signals with real paid SKU-pack evidence before building automation.
